title: One source asset, four platform rewrites: an evidence-first social workflow
core_thesis: Most small teams treat each social platform like a separate content machine and burn time rewriting from scratch.
source_summary: Use one clear source asset, keep the thesis stable, rewrite the hook and pacing per platform, and let platform signal cards shape emphasis instead of unsupported algorithm folklore.
source_text: Most small teams treat each social platform like a separate content machine and burn time rewriting from scratch. A better starting point is one clear source asset with a repeatable rewrite process: keep the core claim stable, adjust the hook and pacing per surface, and let platform-specific ranking signals shape the final version rather than the original idea. The practical workflow is simple: define the thesis, list the strongest proof points, decide what action you want from the reader, then create lean platform-native variants instead of publishing the same post everywhere. This creates a better testing loop and makes it easier to learn what actually travels across platforms without inventing unsupported algorithm myths.
key_points: Most small teams treat each social platform like a separate content machine and burn time rewriting from scratch.

operator_goal: Turn one educational source asset into a multi-platform test package that improves reach, saves, and qualified replies without adding unsupported claims.
primary_cta: Save this workflow, then request the full checklist if you want the reusable planning template.
audience: Small business operators and solo marketers who need a practical repeatable content workflow.
constraints: No sensational claims about platform algorithms.
Keep every recommendation tied to evidence-backed platform signal cards.
No third-party automation, scraping, or outreach language.
Keep the test plan manual and operator-reviewed.
notes: Treat trend suggestions as assumptions, not current-market facts.
Prefer practical hooks over platform slang.
Optimize for reuse from one source asset.
